matter.title: Grace C. Roe v. Maria Q. Fairbanks et al. (member oppression / Riverbend Builders LLC)
matter.summary: Grace C. Roe, who holds a 20% interest in Riverbend Builders LLC, a family restaurant group, alleges that the controlling members Maria Q. Fairbanks and Ingrid B. Kingsley have frozen the minority member out, and sues for oppression, breach of fiduciary duty, and judicial dissolution or a fair-value buyout.

fact_pattern.summary: Grace C. Roe, who holds a 20% interest in Riverbend Builders LLC, a family restaurant group, alleges that the controlling members Maria Q. Fairbanks and Ingrid B. Kingsley have frozen the minority member out, and sues for oppression, breach of fiduciary duty, and judicial dissolution or a fair-value buyout.
fact_pattern.narrative:
Grace C. Roe holds a 20% interest in Riverbend Builders LLC, a family restaurant group organized as a Maine limited liability company. Maria Q. Fairbanks and Ingrid B. Kingsley are the controlling, managing members. The members operate under a written operating agreement, and for years the company made regular distributions to all of its members.

Beginning on or about 2025-03-22, Grace alleges the majority began a coordinated freeze-out, excluding the minority member from management and the company's records and squeezing the minority member out of the business. Distributions to the minority member were suspended even as the controlling members continued to pay themselves substantial salaries, and roughly $804878 in distributions has been withheld. On the question of an exit, no buyout has been offered.

Grace now sues Maria and Ingrid in the Business and Consumer Docket, with the LLC named as a nominal defendant. The complaint seeks judicial dissolution or, in the alternative, a court-ordered buyout at fair value, an accounting for diverted company assets, restored distributions, and access to the company's books and records. The company is valued at roughly $6766345.
undisputed_facts: Grace C. Roe holds a 20% interest in Riverbend Builders LLC.
Maria Q. Fairbanks and Ingrid B. Kingsley are the controlling, managing members of the LLC.
The members operate under a written operating agreement.
Distributions to the minority member have been suspended since on or about 2025-03-22.
disputed_facts: Whether the majority's conduct was oppressive and unfairly prejudicial or instead a legitimate exercise of business judgment.
Whether the controlling members breached the fiduciary duties of loyalty and care owed to the LLC and its members.
The fair value of the minority member's interest and whether a buyout or dissolution is the appropriate remedy.
Whether company opportunities and assets were improperly diverted to the majority's side entity.

timeline.event: Grace is excluded from management and distributions to the minority member are suspended.
Complaint for member oppression and judicial dissolution prepared for filing in the Bath Business and Consumer Docket.

third_parties.name: Olivia Q. Hawthorne
third_parties.relevance: Prepared the company's books, tax returns, and distribution records and can testify to how the majority paid themselves.

third_parties.name: Maine Secretary of State
third_parties.relevance: Maintains the LLC's formation filings, annual reports, and registered-agent records.

third_parties.name: Maria S. Ashford
third_parties.role: Former employee with knowledge of the diversion
third_parties.relevance: Worked at the company and observed opportunities and assets being diverted to the majority's side entity.

third_parties.name: Northstar Partners Corp.
third_parties.role: The side entity allegedly receiving diverted opportunities
third_parties.relevance: An entity controlled by the majority that allegedly received company opportunities and assets.

third_parties.name: Downeast Federal Savings
third_parties.relevance: Holds the LLC's operating accounts; records trace distributions, payroll, and transfers to the side entity.

exchanges.question: Tell me about the company and your stake in it.
exchanges.answer: It's a family restaurant group, set up as a Maine LLC. I own a 20% interest, and Maria and Ingrid are the ones running it. We have a written operating agreement, and for years everybody got paid distributions.

exchanges.question: What changed, and when?
exchanges.answer: Around 2025-03-22 they pushed me out. They stopped my distributions while they kept paying themselves big salaries, cut me off from the books, and shut me out of any say in the business.

exchanges.question: What makes you think company opportunities are being diverted?
exchanges.answer: A former employee told me they've been routing work and assets to a side company that Maria controls. I've been asking to see the books to confirm it, and they keep refusing.

exchanges.question: Has there been any offer to buy you out?
exchanges.answer: On that front, no buyout has been offered. Either way, by my math they've withheld around $804878 in distributions that should have come to me.

exchanges.question: What outcome are you looking for?
exchanges.answer: I want a fair-value buyout of my interest, or dissolution if it comes to that, plus an accounting for whatever they diverted and my distributions restored. I'd settle on fair terms, but I'm prepared to litigate.

goals: Obtain a court-ordered buyout of the minority interest at fair value, or judicial dissolution of the LLC.
Recover an accounting and damages for company opportunities and assets diverted to the majority's side entity.
Restore the suspended distributions owed to the minority member.
Secure full access to the LLC's books and records.
priorities: Establishing the oppressive freeze-out and the controlling members' fiduciary breaches.
Pinning down the fair value of the minority interest.
Tracing the distributions and diverted funds.
desired_outcomes: A fair-value buyout of the plaintiff's interest or dissolution with a fair distribution of value.
An accounting for diverted funds, restored distributions, and access to the company's books.
non_starters: A forced exit at a lowball valuation that ignores the diverted value.

constraints.budget: Funded through trial, but a fair buyout settlement is strongly preferred.
constraints.timeline: Wants the freeze-out addressed promptly and the case trial-ready within roughly eighteen months of filing.

issues.issue: Whether the plaintiff was wrongfully denied the right to inspect the LLC's books and records
sub_questions: Did the plaintiff make a proper demand to inspect?
Was the refusal to allow inspection lawful?
issues.client_position: A proper demand was made and the majority's refusal to allow inspection violates the member's statutory inspection rights.

issues.issue: Whether the managing members breached the fiduciary duties of loyalty and care owed to the LLC and its members
sub_questions: Did the majority self-deal or usurp company opportunities?
Did paying themselves while denying distributions breach the duty of loyalty?
issues.client_position: Paying themselves while freezing out the minority and diverting opportunities breaches the duties of loyalty and care.

issues.issue: Whether a buyout at fair value is available as an alternative remedy to dissolution
sub_questions: May the court order the majority to purchase the minority interest at fair value?
What is the fair value of the minority interest?
issues.client_position: A court-ordered buyout at fair value is an appropriate alternative to dissolution.

issues.issue: Whether the suspension of distributions and the majority's compensation constitute improper distributions
sub_questions: Were distributions withheld from the minority while the majority took disguised distributions as salary?
Were any distributions improper under the Act?
issues.client_position: The majority took disguised distributions through salary while improperly withholding distributions from the minority.

expert_opinions.opinion: Company funds and opportunities were diverted to a related side entity and disguised distributions were paid through salary.
expert_opinions.basis: General ledger, bank records, payroll records, and the side entity's filings.

expert_opinions.topic: Fair value of the minority membership interest
expert_opinions.opinion: The fair value of the minority interest substantially exceeds the majority's buyout offer once diverted value is restored.
expert_opinions.basis: Income and market approaches applied to the company's corrected financials.

evidence.title: Records-inspection demand and refusal
evidence.description: The plaintiff's demand to inspect the books and the majority's refusal.

evidence.title: Emails showing the freeze-out and diversion
evidence.description: Internal emails reflecting the exclusion of the minority member and the diversion of opportunities.

evidence.title: Member meeting minutes and resolutions
evidence.description: Minutes and written resolutions reflecting management decisions and the exclusion of the minority member.

evidence.title: Distribution and payroll records
evidence.description: Records of member distributions and the salaries paid to the controlling members.

evidence.title: Lowball buyout offer
evidence.description: The majority's written offer to purchase the minority interest.

evidence.title: Operating agreement
evidence.description: The LLC's written operating agreement governing management, distributions, and member rights.

evidence.title: Bank records
evidence.description: Bank records tracing distributions, salaries, and transfers to the side entity.

evidence.title: Company financial statements and tax returns
evidence.description: The LLC's financial statements and federal and Maine tax returns for the relevant years.

amounts.label: Fair value of the plaintiff's minority interest
amounts.basis: Pro rata fair value of the membership interest before discounts.

amounts.label: Estimated cost of the valuation and accounting experts
Distributions withheld from the minority member

causes_of_action.against: Maria Q. Fairbanks and Ingrid B. Kingsley
elements: Conduct by the controlling members that is illegal, oppressive, fraudulent, or unfairly prejudicial
Entitlement to dissolution or an alternative remedy
causes_of_action.relief_sought: Judicial dissolution or, in the alternative, a court-ordered buyout at fair value

causes_of_action.against: Maria Q. Fairbanks and Ingrid B. Kingsley
elements: A fiduciary duty of loyalty or care owed to the LLC and its members
causes_of_action.relief_sought: Compensatory damages, disgorgement, and an accounting
Compensatory damages and specific enforcement of the agreement

causes_of_action.claim: Derivative claim for diversion of company assets
causes_of_action.by: Grace C. Roe, derivatively on behalf of Riverbend Builders LLC
elements: Diversion of company opportunities or assets
Recoverable damages on behalf of the company
causes_of_action.relief_sought: Restitution and disgorgement to the LLC

causes_of_action.claim: Demand for inspection of books and records
elements: A purpose reasonably related to the member's interest
causes_of_action.relief_sought: An order compelling inspection of the books and records

affirmative_defenses.basis: The operating agreement authorized the management decisions and compensation at issue.
The plaintiff breached first and comes to equity with unclean hands.
The asserted injuries are to the LLC and may be pursued only derivatively.
The challenged decisions were legitimate, good-faith exercises of business judgment.
The buyout offered to the plaintiff was fair and reasonable.

counterclaims.claim: Breach of fiduciary duty and the operating agreement by the minority member
counterclaims.relief: Damages for the minority member's own alleged misconduct and breaches.

discovery.description: Deposition of the company's outside accountant.
Subpoena to the bank for the LLC's account records.
Plaintiff's valuation and forensic-accounting expert disclosures.
Interrogatories on management, distributions, and the majority's compensation.

motions.title: Motion to compel production of the diversion records
motions.relief: An order compelling production of records relating to the side entity.

motions.title: Motion for partial summary judgment on the fiduciary-duty claim
motions.relief: Judgment that the controlling members breached their fiduciary duties.

motions.title: Motion to appoint a receiver or custodian
motions.relief: Appointment of a receiver or custodian to protect company assets.

motions.title: Motion for a buyout / fair-value determination
motions.relief: An order setting a buyout of the minority interest at fair value.

motions.title: Motion to compel inspection of books and records
motions.relief: An order compelling inspection of the LLC's books and records.

docket.entry: Complaint for member oppression and judicial dissolution filed in the Business and Consumer Docket.
Defendants' answer and counterclaim filed; the LLC appears as a nominal defendant.
Scheduling order entered; discovery deadlines set.
Order granting the motion to compel inspection of books and records.
Order granting in part production of the side entity's records.
Plaintiff's valuation and forensic-accounting expert reports served; discovery continues.
Motion for a buyout / fair-value determination filed.

oppressive_conduct: denial of distributions while the majority take large salaries
refusal to allow inspection of the books
diversion of company opportunities to a side entity
termination of the minority member's employment and salary
